text.title: Type Hint Syntax by Python Version
text.summary: Feature/version matrix, PEP 695 generic syntax (3.12+), type alias statement, and TypedDict evolution.

rule.statement: Before using a typing feature, verify the Python version floor of the repo matches or exceeds the feature's introduction version. Use `from __future__ import annotations` only as a temporary bridge for pre-3.10 syntax on 3.9 codebases.
rule.rationale: Using `X | Y` syntax without `from __future__ import annotations` on Python 3.9 causes `TypeError` at import time. The feature matrix prevents silent syntax breaks in CI or production.

rule.statement: Use PEP 695 syntax on 3.12+ floor: `class Foo[T]:`, `def func[T](x: T) -> T:`, `type Alias = ...`. Migrate legacy `TypeVar`/`Generic` to this form when touching a file.
rule.rationale: PEP 695 eliminates `T = TypeVar('T')` declarations and the `Generic[T]` base class. It co-locates the type parameter with the class/function definition, making the scope unambiguous and removing a common source of TypeVar naming collisions.

example.note: PEP 695 generics â€” class, function, constrained, and alias

rule.statement: Use `TypeIs[T]` (Python 3.13+) instead of `TypeGuard[T]` for narrowing functions when the False branch should also narrow. `TypeGuard` only narrows in the True branch.
rule.rationale: `TypeIs` enables bidirectional narrowing: in `if is_str(x)`, x is `str`; in `else`, x is narrowed to whatever remains after removing `str` from the union. `TypeGuard` leaves the else branch unnarrowed, requiring extra isinstance guards.

example.note: TypeIs bidirectional narrowing vs TypeGuard one-sided

rule.statement: Use class-based TypedDict syntax. Use `NotRequired[T]` for optional fields (Python 3.11+) instead of `total=False` on the whole dict. Use `ReadOnly[T]` (Python 3.13+) for immutable fields.
rule.rationale: `total=False` makes all fields optional, which is too broad. `NotRequired` is field-granular and makes the schema self-documenting. `ReadOnly` expresses intent without needing a frozen dataclass.

example.code:
from typing import TypedDict, NotRequired, ReadOnly
class UserProfile(TypedDict): id: ReadOnly[int] # Cannot be modified after creation email: str # Always required, mutable bio: NotRequired[str] # Optional field avatar_url: NotRequired[str | None]
